assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies the issue as cert-manager's admission webhook causing validation failures due to certificate/API server compatibility issues after the Kubernetes upgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations | grep cert-manager' to identify the problematic webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides immediate fix by patching the webhook with failurePolicy: Ignore and also mentions deleting the webhook entirely as an option

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes 'kubectl delete validatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook' as an emergency option if upgrade fails

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response clearly states this is temporary mitigation and emphasizes restoring webhook security after the permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check current cert-manager version and explicitly mentions checking if it supports Kubernetes 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response advises upgrading to support K8s 1.32 (not 1.30 as specified in expectation), but the expectation appears to have a typo since the user mentioned upgrading to 1.32

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response prominently features patching the webhook with failurePolicy: Ignore as the primary immediate fix before mentioning deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes test commands like 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx' and 'kubectl run verification-pod --image=nginx --dry-run=server' to verify pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the need to persist changes in source of truth systems like Helm charts or GitOps manifests

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states failurePolicy: Ignore is temporary mitigation and includes commands to restore failurePolicy: Fail after the permanent fix is applied
